header_normalization: trim, collapse internal whitespace/newlines to a single space, uppercase
source_file_reference: KRAUS - SALE REPORT-YTD-June.xlsx, sheet 'SALE REPORT' -- client-confirmed 2026-07-18 (WhatsApp: 'Use this, we will use the same format going forward') this is the ongoing export format. Supersedes the original one-off sample (Kraus Sales.xlsx, TRANSACTION DATE/RETAILER/ARTICLE NO/STYLE NAME vocabulary) this brand was onboarded against earlier the same day -- that mapping never had any real data loaded under it (0 fact_sales rows for KRAUS at the time of this change), so this is a clean replacement, not a migration.

unit_mrp.reason: Same derivation as the original Kraus mapping -- this file has no per-unit MRP column either, only a line-total MRP VALUE and QTY SALE. Verified against the real file (2026-07-18): a qty=2 row (LTT-233, net=2160, mrp=3598) divides out to the exact same per-unit net/mrp values as a qty=1 row for the same style (LTT-217, net=1080, mrp=1799), confirming MRP VALUE scales with quantity rather than being a per-unit price. unit_mrp = mrp_value / quantity (apps.ingestion.derivations.unit_mrp_from_mrp_and_quantity).

validation_rules.is_return_when: quantity < 0 OR mrp_value < 0 (verified against the real file: all 24 return rows have both quantity and mrp_value negative together, never just one -- no inconsistent-sign rows found)
notes: This file has TWO sheets -- 'REPORT' (an 8-row pivot summary) and 'SALE REPORT' (the real 718-row transaction detail). sheet_name is set explicitly to 'SALE REPORT': the sheet-name-fallback (falls back to sheet index 0 if the configured name isn't found) would otherwise silently land on 'REPORT' instead, ingesting an 8-row pivot summary rather than the 718 real transactions.

This is a YTD (year-to-date) export -- one file's MONTH column spans January through June 2026 (6 distinct months, verified), not a single month like every other brand's routine upload. Operational consequence worth the client being aware of: since fact_sales replace is scoped by (store, month) slice (ADR-0002), every future YTD upload will re-touch every prior month's slice, which by then already has data -- so ADR-0003's alter_existing_data gate (Super Admin) will fire on every routine Kraus upload from here on, not just genuine corrections, unlike every other brand's single-month day-to-day upload.

sale_date is sourced from CORRECTED DATE, not INVOICE DATE. Verified against the real file: CORRECTED DATE is a clean, 100%-populated datetime value on all 718 rows; INVOICE DATE is inconsistently typed (498 rows a datetime, 220 a string, split across two different string date formats). CORRECTED DATE's name and its consistency both point to it being the client's own reconciled date -- INVOICE DATE is captured to extra for audit only, never used as authoritative.

CATEGORY is genuinely inconsistent in this file -- 59 distinct values across 718 rows, mixing plain garment/fit descriptors (TREGGING, BAGGY, STRAIGHT, WIDE), broader labels with a trailing 'L' (T-SHIRTS L, JEANS L), and season-prefixed labels (S26 KRAUS T-SHIRT, W25 KRAUS TREGGING) -- sometimes all three styles appear for the exact same ITEM NAME prefix (e.g. every LFA- row is some mix of JEANS L / WIDE / STRAIGHT / L DENIM / FLARE). Mapped as-supplied anyway, same as every other brand's category (never re-classified on our side), but worth flagging back to the client if Kraus's category-based reporting looks fragmented -- it traces to their own export, not this pipeline.

category, store_name and color (SHADE) are NOT in required_canonical_fields here, unlike the original mapping (which required category and store_name) -- the real file has a small number of genuinely blank cells for category (2/718) and color (4/718) with no sensible default, and this now matches the same required-field baseline used by Killer/Pepe/Junior Killer/Pepe Kids rather than the original mapping's one-off deviation from it.

No supplied discount-percentage column exists in this file at all (unlike the original mapping's TOTAL DISCOUNT PCT, or Pepe's WAD) -- the discount-percent sanity cross-check only runs if a supplied value is present (apps.ingestion.validation), so this is simply omitted rather than needing a placeholder.

EAN CODE has trailing non-breaking-space padding on a meaningful minority of rows (191/718, e.g. '8905747548711\xa0\xa0\xa0...') -- confirmed the existing barcode caster's str.strip() already strips \xa0 along with ordinary whitespace, so this needed no code change, just verification that it was actually handled.

STORE NAME has an inconsistent-casing duplicate ('PANKH' vs 'Pankh', same STORE CODE 'KRA-1') -- confirmed harmless: store identity resolves by STORE CODE alone (apps.ingestion.dimension_resolver.resolve_stores), and store_name is one of row_mapper's TEXT_DIMENSION_FIELDS, uppercased unconditionally regardless of this config -- both variants normalize to the same 'PANKH' before a store row is ever created.
INVOICE NO is mixed int/str in the raw file (382/336 rows respectively), same convention as Pepe's BillNo and Killer's Bill No -- always cast to string, never numeric.

This one file shows 3 distinct (STORE CODE, STORE NAME) identities: KRA-1/PANKH, KRA-2/DAFTARI TEXTILES PVT LTD, KRA-3/THE BOMBAY FASHION -- Kraus appears to sell through a small reseller/distributor network rather than only its own stores.
